message: fix(pt-core): harden lifecycle and container/escalation semantics (bd-1ueq)

files.patch:
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1rf2","title":"E2E: session diff + compare workflows","description":"## Goal
Exercise session diff/compare paths end-to-end with JSONL logs and negative-path validation.

## Scope
- `pt diff --last` on two sessions from fixtures.
- `pt agent diff` with JSON output verification.
- Failure paths: missing session, invalid session id, schema mismatch.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.
- Validate diff summary fields and delta counts.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs with command, session_ids, delta_summary, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: diff outputs (json + human), validation report, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Diff outputs schema-validate and are deterministic.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted for each diff path.

## Test Plan
- E2E script creates two fixture sessions and runs diff commands.

 {"id":"bd-1sj4","title":"No-mock policy enforcement (denylist/allowlist + CI gate)","description":"## Goal
Enforce a no-mock policy for core modules with automated checks.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Define denylist of mock crates/usages for core modules (collect, inference, decision, plan, action).
- Allowlist in test-only helpers and explicitly mock-friendly modules.
- CI job to fail if denylisted mocks appear in restricted paths.
- Provide lint/test helper to scan for mock usage patterns.
- Document the no-mock policy and exception process.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Denylist/allowlist defined and documented.
- [ ] CI gate fails on forbidden mock usage in core modules.
- [ ] Local check command documented.

## Test Plan
- Unit: policy parser/validator for denylist rules.
- Integration: intentionally introduce mock usage in a fixture and verify CI fails.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:00:08.269672186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:09:16.818654162Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:09:16.818361398Z","close_reason":"Implemented no-mock policy check, docs, CI gate","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sj4","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:00:08.282524208Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tes","title":"E2E: daemon/dormant monitoring with JSONL logs","description":"## Goal
Add E2E tests for dormant daemon workflows with rich logging.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Start daemon, run periodic scans, emit events.
- Validate inbox/escalation outputs.
- Clean shutdown and restart resiliency.
- JSONL logs + process snapshots stored as artifacts.
- Safety: verify daemon never performs destructive actions in test mode.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, session_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact manifest includes daemon logs, snapshots, and session artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Daemon E2E runs deterministically in CI.
- [ ] Logs capture lifecycle events and scan summaries.
- [ ] Restart behavior validated.
- [ ] JSONL schema validation passes.

## Test Plan
- E2E: daemon start -> scan -> event -> shutdown -> restart.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:25.042975788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:28:47.315150545Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T09:28:47.315087518Z","close_reason":"Completed: added daemon monitoring BATS E2E + manifest validation (see test/daemon_dormant_e2e.bats)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tes","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:23.346053540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tes","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:25.054877049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tes","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:32.724315475Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tes","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:25.060790060Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tes","depends_on_id":"process_triage-b4v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:25.065976921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1ueq","title":"Fix lifecycle overflow + session summary accuracy + k8s docker detection","description":"Deep review found reliability defects: (1) session lifecycle  reports stale state transition count, (2) TTL arithmetic uses unchecked u64->i64 conversions in create/extend paths, (3) k8s cgroup paths with docker segment are classified as docker before kubernetes metadata extraction, (4) escalation notifications use detection timestamp for single trigger rendering instead of flush timestamp. Add regression tests and patch behavior.","notes":"Claimed by OrangeSpring for deep cross-agent bugfix sweep; implementing fixes + regression tests in lifecycle/container/escalation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OrangeSpring","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:04:26.383276306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:07:36.754723234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:07:36.754634850Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed lifecycle transition counting and TTL overflow guards; corrected kubernetes docker-path classification; aligned escalation notification created_at semantics; added regression tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ueq","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:04:26.396654525Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uey","title":"E2E: agent tail, watch --once command tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderBasin","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:06:57.186468811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T04:13:51.465251145Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T04:13:51.465121734Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uey","depends_on_id":"process_triage-5q2m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:06:57.196023921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v91","title":"No-mock output formatting + token-efficient pipeline tests","description":"## Goal
Validate output formatting and token-efficient paths using real data structures (no mocks), with E2E coverage provided in bd-1o51.

## Scope
- Unit tests for FieldSelector, CompactConfig, token truncation metadata, and TOON encoding.
- Integration tests that run real scan/plan outputs through formatter (JSON + TOON).
- Golden outputs for representative payloads (scan + plan + explain) with deterministic ordering.
- Provide fixtures and expectations consumed by E2E output-mode tests (bd-1o51).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL per test case: event, timestamp, case_id, format, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Store goldens under `test/fixtures/output/` with checksum tracking.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No-mock unit coverage for FieldSelector + CompactConfig + TOON.
- [ ] Integration coverage for scan/plan output formatting (JSON + TOON).
- [ ] Golden outputs stable and updated only via explicit approval.
- [ ] Fixtures + logs are consumable by bd-1o51 E2E script.

## Test Plan
- Unit: direct structs -> formatter -> expected output.
- Integration: run pt-core formatting on real outputs from fixture sessions.
